summary: TestURISchemeAllowed tests urischeme package in app_test; belongs in urischeme_test.go
detail: TestURISchemeAllowed calls urischeme.AllowedOn(url, "linux") directly. The app uses urischeme.Allowed(rawURL) which delegates to AllowedOn(rawURL, runtime.GOOS). This test duplicates urischeme_test.go coverage and lives in the wrong package. urischeme_test.go already has comprehensive AllowedOn tests including all cases in TestURISchemeAllowed.
suggestion: Remove TestURISchemeAllowed from app_test.go. If app-level URL validation needs coverage, add a test that exercises OpenURL with blocked URLs (validation path only, no openBrowser call).

summary: OpenURL has no tests; blocked-URL path is testable without Wails
detail: OpenURL(rawURL) validates via urischeme.Allowed() and returns early when blocked (lines 148–150). When blocked, openBrowser is never called, so the validation path can be tested without a Wails context. No tests exist for OpenURL. OpenHelp delegates to OpenURL (line 144) and is also untested.
suggestion: Add TestOpenURLBlocked: call a.OpenURL("javascript:alert(1)"), a.OpenURL("file:///etc/passwd"), a.OpenURL("") and verify no panic. App has nil ctx before Startup; blocked path never reaches openBrowser. For OpenHelp, add TestOpenHelpDelegatesToOpenURL: set HelpURL to blocked URL, call OpenHelp, verify no panic (OpenHelp skips when empty; when set, delegates to OpenURL which blocks).

detail: GetCheckState() returns a copy of checkState (lines 157–165). New() initializes checkState via loadCheckState() which reads from marker.Dir()/checklist.json. No tests verify GetCheckState returns correct initial state or that the returned map is a copy (mutations don't affect internal state).
suggestion: Add TestGetCheckState: create app, assert GetCheckState() returns empty map (or expected state). Call ToggleCheckItem("key1"), assert GetCheckState()["key1"] == true. Mutate the returned map and assert GetCheckState()["key1"] still true (copy semantics). Use os.Setenv("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", t.TempDir()) before New() to avoid polluting real config.

detail: ToggleCheckItem(key) toggles checkState[key], persists via saveCheckState, and returns the new value (lines 167–174). No tests cover toggle semantics, persistence, or return value. saveCheckState writes to marker.Dir()/checklist.json; tests would need XDG_CONFIG_HOME override to avoid polluting user config.
suggestion: Add TestToggleCheckItem: set XDG_CONFIG_HOME to t.TempDir(), create app. Assert ToggleCheckItem("a") == true (false->true), ToggleCheckItem("a") == false (true->false). Create new App (New), assert GetCheckState()["a"] == false (persistence round-trip).

detail: loadCheckState returns empty map when checklistPath() is "" (marker.Dir fails), when ReadFile fails, or when Unmarshal fails (lines 221–235). saveCheckState no-ops when checklistPath is "" (lines 239–242). These error paths affect robustness but are unexported; coverage would require marker.Dir() to fail or corrupt file.
suggestion: Consider table-driven test with XDG_CONFIG_HOME unset (or invalid) to hit checklistPath empty path, or use a read-only dir to trigger write failure. Lower priority since these are defensive paths.

summary: testApp does not set XDG_CONFIG_HOME; checklist tests may pollute user config
detail: testApp(n, cfg) calls New() which calls loadCheckState(). ToggleCheckItem triggers saveCheckState() writing to marker.Dir() (e.g. ~/.config/day1/checklist.json). Any new tests for GetCheckState/ToggleCheckItem that don't set XDG_CONFIG_HOME will read/write real user config.
suggestion: Add helper testAppInTempDir(t, n, cfg) that sets XDG_CONFIG_HOME to t.TempDir() and restores it in defer. Use for checklist-related tests.
